def: A close female friend, or a woman someone is romantically dating.
The third-person pronoun for a female person, or for plural 'they'.
Joins two words, phrases, or clauses of equal weight.
Introduces an alternative between two or more choices.
The point of twelve o'clock at night, when one day ends and the next begins.
Occurring near the start of a period, before the expected or usual time.
Occurring after the expected time, or near the end of the day.
The pronoun used to address more than one person informally, meaning 'you all'.
The possessive word for something belonging to me.
The possessive word for something belonging to you (informal).
The possessive word for something belonging to us.
The possessive word for something belonging to you all.
A pointing word meaning 'this', referring to someone or something close by.
A pointing word meaning 'that', referring to someone or something farther away.
The reflexive pronoun used when a person or thing does something to themselves.
The object form of 'you' (informal), used when you are the one being affected.
The object form of 'I', used when I am the one being affected.
The object form of 'he', used for a masculine person or thing being affected directly.
The indirect-object form of 'he', used for a masculine person or thing receiving something.
The indirect-object form of 'they' or formal 'you', used for the person receiving something.
The object form of 'we', used when we are being affected or receiving something.
The object form of 'you all', used when you all are being affected or receiving something.
A word meaning 'whose' or 'their', referring back to a feminine or plural noun already mentioned.
A phrase said to wish someone good sleep before bed.
A casual way to say goodbye when you expect to see someone again soon.
A casual way to ask how someone is feeling.
A common reply meaning that you feel fine or well.
A word used to greet someone warmly on their arrival.
A polite phrase said when meeting someone for the first time formally.
A polite phrase used when handing something over, or replying to thanks.
An enthusiastic phrase used to welcome someone warmly.
A way to say goodbye when you expect to see someone again the next day.
A phrase said before eating to wish someone an enjoyable meal.
For water to fall from clouds in drops.
A two-wheeled vehicle you ride by pushing pedals with your feet.
Used to give the reason or cause behind something.
Indicates that something or someone is missing or not included.
Indicates who or what something is intended or meant for.
Indicates opposition to something, or physical contact against it.
Indicates a time coming after something, or movement toward a place.
Indicates a time coming before something, or a position in front of it.
Indicates being located at, close to, or with someone or something.
A marked track, lane, or path that something moves or travels along.
The last part or final point of something.
Precisely correct, with no mistake or difference from what's expected.
A polite reply meaning you were glad to help; like 'you're welcome'.
The record and study of events from humanity's past.
Being the final one in a series, or the most recent one.
Limits something to just the amount, thing, or option mentioned.
An individual human being, referred to as a single person.
Used before an adjective or adverb to mean 'to a high degree'.
The Earth, along with all its people and everything on it.
Relating to England, its people, or the English language.
Equal to one of two same-sized parts of something whole.
Used to express agreement or to say something is acceptable.
Having a large width, or having a lot of body fat; not thin.
A written list of items you intend to buy.
